strengths: Three specific, concrete realâworld objects are clearly identified, satisfying the basic requirement of the task.
The personal narrative about seeking reproductive health knowledge is engaging and effectively shows how lived experience can drive inquiry.
Ambitious attempt to generalize a leadership experience (the fries object) into social knowledge, demonstrating initiative in linking personal experience to knowledge.
weaknesses: Contextual details (dates, precise locations) for each object are often vague or missing, weakening the sense of realâworld grounding.
Commentary remains largely descriptive and narrative, with very limited use of TOK terminology (e.g., ways of knowing, knowledge frameworks) or analytical depth.
The third objectâs link to the chosen prompt is thin and anecdotal; there is no explicit formulation of a knowledge question or sufficient supporting evidence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student meets the basic descriptor for band 3â4 by identifying three concrete objects and providing some justification for their inclusion. There is clear evidence of personal experience shaping knowledge, particularly in the contraceptive example, but the overall analysis is superficial: real-world contexts lack specificity, TOK concepts are rarely invoked, and the third objectâs link to the prompt is underdeveloped. On balance, the work aligns with the upper end of the 3â4 band, warranting a final score of 4.

annotations.comment: To deepen the analysis, reference specific ways of knowing (e.g. language, emotion) to illustrate how the painting experience shaped your understanding.

The commentary names a personal experience with PlanB but omits precise contextual details such as the date and specific location. Including these would strengthen the real-world context of the object.
The student should explicitly state the chosen IA prompt at the beginning of the commentary to anchor the analysis and ensure clarity of focus.
The reference to the emergency contraceptive packet is a strong choice: it is concrete and immediately conveys a real-world object linked to personal experience.
Consider integrating specific TOK terminology (e.g. âways of knowing,â âperspectivesâ) when discussing how the experience shaped your understanding.
The figure lacks a descriptive caption linking the image explicitly to the IA prompt. A clear label would reinforce the connection between object and knowledge question.
The narrative in this section is repetitive and predominantly descriptive, offering limited analytical depth into how experience informs knowledge.
To deepen the analysis, explicitly discuss how your emotional response led to specific knowledge gains, using TOK concepts such as emotion and reason.
What is the purpose of the lone "S" in this section? It disrupts flow and appears to be an OCR artifact or typo.
The header "Commentary 2" appears abruptly without a transitional sentence to orient the reader to the second objectâs analysis.
The description âImage of object 2â is too generic. Specify what the image shows and how it relates to the prompt.
The painting image lacks a caption describing its significance. A caption should identify the object, context, and its link to the selected prompt.
The context for the elementary art competition is vagueâno date or location is provided. Adding these details would ground the object in its realâworld setting.
Explicitly connect the paintingâs narrative to the knowledge question about the limitations of oneâs worldview to clarify its relevance.
This section lacks mention of TOK terminology such as âperspectivesâ or âknowledge frameworksâ when reflecting on how your worldview expanded.
Why is âCommentary 3â presented here without accompanying analysis? Ensure each header is followed by substantive commentary.
The fries image lacks any caption or context. Identify the object clearly and explain its relevance to the IA prompt.
The paragraph begins midâsentence, affecting coherence. Ensure each section starts with a complete thought.
This final commentary is rich in narrative but offers little evidence or external references to support your generalizations about social navigation.
To clarify relevance, explicitly formulate a knowledge question for the fries object and demonstrate how your leadership experience generated knowledge.
Provide external evidence or references (e.g. studies on team leadership) to support broader claims about navigating social situations.
The personal recounting of seeking reproductive health knowledge is engaging and effectively illustrates how lived experience can drive inquiry.
The anecdote about the surf painting compellingly demonstrates how early experiences can expand the boundaries of oneâs knowledge bubble.
The fries example shows an attempt to generalize a personal leadership experience into social knowledge, indicating good ambition.
State the IA prompt heading at the top of the first page to orient the reader immediately to your chosen question.
Could you cite specific sources for the statistics on reproductive rights (e.g. the 270 million figure) to substantiate your claims?
How did you evaluate the credibility of the sources you used for your reproductive health research? Discuss source reliability.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
